stemHtml:
You are the PM of a commercial jet airliner flying an ILS CATIII approach in fail-operational status. If, at 70 feet RA, the G/S indication disappears, can you continue the approach? What would the operational state be? If there's a warning, what type of warning would you get?

Alert height: 100 ft
RVR: 75 m

options.a: The crew receives an autoland warning and the approach may be continued in fail-operational status.
options.b: The crew receives a caution alert and the approach cannot be continued because the FLARE mode would not engage.
options.c: The crew receives a warning and a go-around must be made.
options.d: The crew receives no warning and the approach can be continued in fail-passive status.
